In [4]:
# Import all of the necessary libraries for visualizations and analysis
import numpy as np
import pandas as pd
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import seaborn as sns
import warnings
warnings.filterwarnings('ignore')

Phase 1: Data Analysis and Preparation¶

dataset = pd.read_csv('secondary_data.csv', delimiter=';')
dataset.head()

Step 1: Clean the Features and Handle Missing Data

Feature 1: cap-diameter

In [8]:
# check for missing values
print(f"Missing Values: {dataset['cap-diameter'].isnull().sum()}")
print(f"Percentage Missing: {dataset['cap-diameter'].isnull().sum()/len(dataset['cap-diameter'])}")

Missing Values: 0
Percentage Missing: 0.0
In [9]:
# Print the summary statistics for this  numerical feature
desc = dataset['cap-diameter'].describe()
formatted_desc = desc.apply(lambda x: f"{x:.3f}")
print(formatted_desc)

count    61069.000
mean         6.734
std          5.265
min          0.380
25%          3.480
50%          5.860
75%          8.540
max         62.340
Name: cap-diameter, dtype: object
In [10]:
# Plot a histogram of the feature
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
sns.histplot(dataset['cap-diameter'], bins=30, kde=True)
plt.title('Distribution of Cap-Diameter')
plt.xlabel('Cap-Diameter')
plt.ylabel('Frequency')
Out[10]:
Text(0, 0.5, 'Frequency')
In [11]:
# Normalize the right skewed data using z-score
dataset['cap-diameter'] = (dataset['cap-diameter'] - dataset['cap-diameter'].mean()) / dataset['cap-diameter'].std()
# Plot the normalized figure
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
sns.histplot(dataset['cap-diameter'], bins=30, kde=True)
plt.title('Distribution of Cap-Diameter (Normalized)')
plt.xlabel('Cap-Diameter')
plt.ylabel('Frequency')
Out[11]:
Text(0, 0.5, 'Frequency')

Feature 2: Cap-shape

In [13]:
# Check for missing values
print(f"Missing Values: {dataset['cap-shape'].isnull().sum()}")
print(f"Percentage Missing: {dataset['cap-shape'].isnull().sum()/len(dataset['cap-shape'])}")

Missing Values: 0
Percentage Missing: 0.0
In [14]:
# Plot the histogram of the cap_shape (show the actual cap shape bell, etc.)
labels2 = ['bell', 'conical', 'convex', 'flat', 'knobbed', 'sunken']
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
sns.histplot(dataset['cap-shape'], bins=30, kde=True)
plt.title('Distribution of Cap-Shape')
plt.xlabel('Cap-Shape')
plt.ylabel('Frequency')
plt.xticks(range(6), labels2, rotation=45)
plt.show()
In [15]:
# Convert the categorical data to numeric form
dataset = pd.get_dummies(dataset, columns=['cap-shape'], dtype = 'int')

plt.title('Distribution of Does Bruise or Bleed')

Feature 6: Gill Attachment

In [28]:
# Check for missing values
print(f"Missing Values: {dataset['gill-attachment'].isnull().sum()}")
print(f"Percentage Missing: {dataset['gill-attachment'].isnull().sum()/len(dataset['gill-attachment'])}")

Missing Values: 9884
Percentage Missing: 0.16184971098265896
In [29]:
# Fill NA with 'f' - assume None
dataset['gill-attachment'] = dataset['gill-attachment'].fillna('f')
In [30]:
print(f"Missing Values: {dataset['gill-attachment'].isnull().sum()}")

Missing Values: 0
In [31]:
# Combine f and ? into just f
dataset['gill-attachment'] = dataset['gill-attachment'].replace('?', 'f')
In [32]:
# Plot the histogram
labels6 = ['free', 'none/unknown', 'adnate', 'decurrent', 'sinuate', 'adnexed', 'pores']
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))
sns.histplot(dataset['gill-attachment'], bins=30, kde=True)
plt.xticks(range(7), labels6, rotation=45)
plt.title('Distribution of Gill Attachment')

Feature 19: Habitat

In [71]:
# check for missing values
print(f"Missing Values: {dataset['habitat'].isnull().sum()}")
print(f'Percentage Missing: {dataset["habitat"].isnull().sum()/len(dataset["habitat"])}')

Missing Values: 0
Percentage Missing: 0.0
In [72]:
# Map the features
dataset['habitat'] = dataset['habitat'].map({'g': 1, 'l': 2, 'm': 3, 'p': 4, 'h': 5, 'u': 6, 'w': 7, 'd': 8})
In [73]:
# Map them back
dataset['habitat'] = dataset['habitat'].map({1: 'g', 2: 'l', 3: 'm', 4: 'p', 5: 'h', 6: 'u', 7: 'w', 8: 'd'})
In [74]:
labels18 = ['grasses', 'leaves', 'meadows', 'paths', 'heaths', 'urban', 'waste', 'woods']
plt.figure(figsize=(10, 6))

# Plot the histogram with explicit bins for exact alignment
bin_edges = range(len(labels18) + 1)  # 0 to 8
sns.histplot(dataset['habitat'], bins=bin_edges, discrete=True)

# Adjust xticks to align with bin centers
bin_centers = [i + 1 for i in range(len(labels18))]  # Center of each bin
plt.xticks(bin_centers, labels18, rotation=45)

plt.title('Distribution of Habitat')
plt.xlabel('Habitat')
plt.ylabel('Frequency')
plt.show()
In [75]:
# Change to numeric variables using pd.get_dummies
dataset = pd.get_dummies(dataset, columns=['habitat'], dtype = 'int')

# check for missing values
print(f"Missing Values: {dataset['class'].isnull().sum()}")

Missing Values: 0
In [80]:
# p to 1 and e to 0
dataset['class'] = dataset['class'].map({'p': 1, 'e': 0})
In [81]:
counts = dataset['class'].value_counts(normalize=True)
absolute_counts = dataset['class'].value_counts()
bins = [0, 1]  # Adjust if there are different class labels

# Create the histogram
plt.hist(dataset['class'], bins=2, rwidth=0.8, align='mid', color='skyblue', edgecolor='black')
plt.xticks(range(2), ['edible', 'poisonous'])
plt.title('Distribution of Class')
plt.xlabel('Class')
plt.ylabel('Frequency')

# Add text for the normalized values and absolute counts above each bar
for i, count in enumerate(absolute_counts):
    normalized_value = counts[i]
    if i == 0:
      plt.text(
          bins[i] + 0.25,  # x-coordinate for text
          count -5000,  # y-coordinate (slightly above the bar)
          f"{normalized_value:.2%}",  # Convert normalized value to percentage format
          ha='center', fontsize=10, color='black'
      )
    else:
      plt.text(
          bins[i] - 0.25,  # x-coordinate for text
          count + 7000,  # y-coordinate (slightly above the bar)
          f"{normalized_value:.2%}",  # Convert normalized value to percentage format
          ha='center', fontsize=10, color='black'
      )

plt.show()
